title: Fig. 1 Mathematics   137
subject: Mathematics
objective:
To find the relationship between areas
and sides of similar triangles.
Coloured papers, glue, geometry
box, scissors/cutters, white paper.
procedure:
1. Take a coloured paper of size 15 cm × 15 cm.
2. On a white paper, draw a triangle ABC.
3. Divide the side AB of   ∆ ABC into some equal parts [say 4 parts].
4. Through the points of division, draw line segments parallel to BC and through
points of division of AC, draw line segments parallel to AB [see Fig. 1].
observation:
By actual measurement:
PE = ___________,   PF = ___________,   EQ = ___________,
FR = ___________
PE
EQ   = _______,   PF
FR   = ___________
Thus,   PE   PF
EQ   FR
=   . It verifies the Theorem.
discussion:
The theorem can be used to establish various criteria of similarity of triangles.
It can also be used for constructing a polygon similar to a given polygon with a
given scale factor.
Mathematics   139
